notes: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .

This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. However, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division courses as possible from the " Recommended Preparation " section below to support timely degree completion. Students applying to non-screening majors are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements prior to transfer. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.

Prior to the 2025-2026 academic year, the Ancient Studies B.A. was offered under the name Classical Studies B.A. Although not required for admission, prospective transfer students interested in this major are encouraged to complete the articulated equivalent of the following course prior to transfer: ONE lower-division survey of ancient history or literature in translation Refer to the " Recommended Preparation " section below for courses approved to satisfy the survey of ancient history or literature in translation requirement. Transfer students can complete the Ancient Studies major within two years, provided they successfully complete GREE 1 – Elementary Ancient Greek or LATN 1 – Elementary Latin during their first quarter at UC Santa Cruz. These courses are offered only in the fall quarter. Students with prior knowledge of ancient Greek or Latin should consult with Ancient Studies faculty to determine whether they have satisfied any elementary language course requirements. Transfer applicants seeking winter-quarter entry will be screened by the program to verify prior knowledge of ancient Greek or Latin. To schedule this screening, please contact the Ancient Studies Director . Additional Information for Transfer Some background in history and in literary analysis and interpretation is desirable, but not essential. Since this is an interdisciplinary major, preparatory work in art history, history, literature, philosophy, or politics, depending on the student's interests, may also be useful. Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor. Visit the Ancient Studies website to learn more about this major.
